~ @TcC| Consensus Premium | & ™
_ January 17 | HIGH-CONFIDENCE PICK
NBA (7:30pm ES~-
Celtics -3.5 (-110) = 2.5U
The Collective Analysis
(Combined reasoning from top cappers)
« Defense vs. Defense Gap: Boston ranks
second in points allowed and third in opponent
field goal percentage. Atlanta ranks 25th in
points allowed and 23rd in field goal
percentage allowed. That efficiency gap is
significant, especially with Boston also ranking
fifth in rebounds allowed against a Hawks team
: that sits 25th in total rebounds and 26th in
offensive rebounds.
¢ Turnover Discipline: Boston is first in the
NBA in turnovers per game, while Atlanta ranks
only 16th and just gave up 24 points off 18
turnovers to Portland. The Celtics are also the
best team in the league at limiting points off
turnovers, directly shutting down one of
Atlanta’s few high-impact scoring paths.
¢ Scoring Stability and Depth: Jaylen Brown is
averaging 29.4 points on 58.5 TS%, facing a
defense near the bottom of the league. Boston
is second in offensive rating despite no single
dominant counting stat, showing reliable
scoring balance. Atlanta is 19th in offensive
rating and has struggled recently with
_ turnovers and rebounding, which favors .
Boston maintaining control and covering the \
3.5-point spread. © 13:42 PM ae
